content.text:
Here’s what was implemented.

### `PlotLocationMap` (`apps/frontend-web/src/components/plot-location-map.tsx`)

- **Leaflet + react-leaflet**, same stack as the camping explorer (`MapContainer`, `TileLayer`, `CircleMarker`, `Tooltip`).
- **SSR-safe**: shows “Loading map…” until `useEffect` runs on the client (same pattern as `explore-map.tsx`).
- **Animated moves**: after the first paint (`setView` without animation), updates use **`map.flyTo(..., { duration })`** so pan/zoom animates when the selected plot or its coordinates change.
- **Plot switches**: a `transitionKey` of `` `${plotId}:${lat}:${lng}:${zoom}` `` drives the effect so a **new plot** still triggers `flyTo` even if coordinates match another plot (unlikely but covered).
- **Configurable props**: `zoom`, `flyToDurationSeconds`, `markerRadius`, `markerLabel`, optional `tileLayer` (`url` + `attribution`), `className`.
- **`MapResizeSync`**: `ResizeObserver` + `invalidateSize`, matching the camping map.

### `plot-detail-panel.tsx`

- Replaced the Google **iframe** with `<PlotLocationMap plotId={plot.id} lat={lat} lng={lng} markerLabel={…} />` when coordinates exist.
- Left the **Open in Google Maps** button and the no-coordinates fallback unchanged.

### Dependencies

- Added **`leaflet`**, **`react-leaflet`**, and **`@types/leaflet`** to `@portugal/frontend-web`.

`bun run build` for the app completes successfully. To tune the map from the panel later, pass props such as `zoom`, `flyToDurationSeconds`, or `tileLayer`.

[REDACTED]

Leaflet loads at module evaluation time, so a static import pulls it into the SSR bundle. Adding a thin client wrapper that dynamically imports the map module only in `useEffect` (after mount) so the server never executes Leaflet.

[REDACTED]

Creating a client-only loader that dynamically imports the Leaflet module inside `useEffect` so SSR never loads Leaflet. Removing the redundant inner `mounted` gate from `PlotLocationMap`.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd /Users/psoudan/workspace/11-personal/portugal/apps/frontend-web && bunx biome format --write src/panels/plot-detail-panel.tsx src/components/plot-location-map-client.tsx && bunx biome check src/components/plot-location-map.tsx src/components/plot-location-map-client.tsx src/panels/plot-detail-panel.tsx && bun run build
input.description: Format panel, re-run biome check and build

content.text:
Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
`leaflet` touches `window` as soon as the module loads. Importing `PlotLocationMap` from `plot-detail-panel` meant **Node SSR loaded that module** during dev/build, so Leaflet ran on the server even though the inner `mounted` flag only delayed React rendering.

### Fix
1. **`plot-location-map-client.tsx`** – SSR-safe entry:
   - No static imports of `./plot-location-map`, Leaflet, or react-leaflet.
   - Only `import type { PlotLocationMapProps } from "./plot-location-map"` (types are erased at compile time).
   - In **`useEffect`**, `import("./plot-location-map")` runs **only in the browser** after mount, then renders `PlotLocationMap`.

2. **`plot-detail-panel.tsx`** – Uses **`PlotLocationMapClient`** instead of `PlotLocationMap`.

3. **`plot-location-map.tsx`** – Removed the extra `mounted` placeholder; the client wrapper owns the loading state until the async chunk loads.

Production build now splits Leaflet into a **~155 kB client chunk** while the **SSR `plot-location-map` asset stays ~3 kB** (no Leaflet in that path).

Use **`PlotLocationMapClient`** anywhere you need the map from code that runs on the server; keep **`PlotLocationMap`** for client-only routes or tests that import the real map directly.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
I followed **`skills/git-commits/SKILL.md`**: checked the real tree and diff before deciding what to commit.

### Portugal repo (`11-personal/portugal`)

- **There is nothing left to commit.** Tracked files match `HEAD`; no staged or unstaged changes.
- Your branch is **ahead of `origin/main` by 3 commits**; the Leaflet plot map + client-only loader and related frontend work are already in history (including under `feat(frontend-web): expand plot workspace with dashboards and panels`).
- The only pending paths are **untracked** under `packages/ui-lib/storybook-static/`. Per the commit skill, that’s **build/static output** and should **not** be committed unless you explicitly treat Storybook deploy artifacts as versioned (usually they belong in `.gitignore` or CI artifacts).

**Suggested next step:** publish the existing commits with `git push` when you’re ready.

### Other workspaces

- **Procertus / camping** still have large, separate dirty trees; those were **not** part of this Portugal map session and were **not** committed here.

If you want `storybook-static` ignored going forward, say so and we can add an ignore rule in a follow-up change.
